title: Miller Orbit PASS: 21 minerals, 13055 reflections, 4 crystal systems — all structural predictions confirmed
_fixture_purpose: Batch witness. Verifies all four structural predictions of QA mod-9 orbit classification applied to Miller indices across 21 minerals spanning cubic, hexagonal, tetragonal, and orthorhombic crystal systems.

ordering.claim: cosmos mean d-spacing > satellite mean d-spacing for ALL minerals and crystal systems
ordering.proof: Satellite requires |h|,|k| both divisible by m//3 = 3 (mod-9). This forces h²+k² >= 9, so Q_M = h²+k²+l² >= 9. Since d = a/sqrt(Q_M) for cubic (and analogous for other systems), higher Q_M → smaller d.

quadratic_residues.claim: Satellite Q_M mod 9 is restricted to quadratic residues QR(9) = {0, 1, 4, 7}
quadratic_residues.proof: Satellite has h,k both divisible by 3, so h² + k² ≡ 0 mod 9. Therefore Q_M = h² + k² + l² ≡ l² mod 9, which is always a quadratic residue mod 9.

perfect_squares.claim: Singularity Q_M values are always perfect squares
perfect_squares.proof: Singularity has h = k = 0 (both mapped to m=9 in QA mod), so Q_M = 0² + 0² + l² = l².

chromogeometric_shift.claim: Satellite has higher green-channel fraction than cosmos
chromogeometric_shift._note: Green channel = Qg = 2hk dominates when h ≈ k. Satellite divisibility by 3 creates more h=k=3n coincidences.
